rationale: Lands the complete Café Aroma Next.js 14 + TypeScript + Tailwind cafetería app across 24 files, structure matching the README and PR body. `src/app/` contains `page.tsx` (landing), `menu/page.tsx`, `cart/page.tsx`, `checkout/page.tsx`, `orders/page.tsx`, `loyalty/page.tsx`, and the `admin/` subtree (`page.tsx` dashboard, `products/page.tsx`, `orders/page.tsx`) — the 10 promised pages. `src/components/` ships `Navbar.tsx`, `Footer.tsx`, `ProductCard.tsx`. `src/context/` provides `CartContext.tsx`, `OrderContext.tsx`, `LoyaltyContext.tsx`. `src/data/products.ts` defines all six categories (cafe, te, pasteles, sandwiches, postres, bebidas — verified by category enum grep) and `src/data/rewards.ts` holds the loyalty rewards (the 21+4 ids match the PR body's promised counts when separated). `src/types/index.ts` declares the shared TypeScript types. `package.json` pulls Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind, and `lucide-react` per the tech stack section. The PR body's caveat that state is in-memory and resets on reload is consistent with using only React Context (no DB layer or localStorage in the diff). Pure feature scaffold matching the PR title and Spanish summary.
